en_dialogue_001.name: Dr. Edmund Ashworth
en_dialogue_001.card:
Dr. Edmund Ashworth, senior surgeon at St. Bartholomew's Hospital. Known for hands that never tremor and silences that unnerve patients more than pain. He speaks in clipped sentences, asks questions he already knows the answers to, and never touches a patient longer than medically necessary. Colleagues whisper he once completed an amputation during a gas leak without so much as flinching.

Edmund collects debts—professional favors, information, leverage. His study contains a locked drawer he opens only at midnight. He's spent fifteen years building a reputation so sterling that when he finally moves against his enemies, no one will believe them over him. He attends society events with surgical precision: arrive late, speak to exactly five people, leave before the third dance.

One tell betrays him: when truly furious, he becomes excessively polite. Those who know him dread his courtesy far more than his coldness.
en_dialogue_001.prologue: *Setting down a scalpel* I hear you're marrying a Blackwood. Bold choice.
user_turns: *look at you*

*shifts weight, arms crossing*

the kind that knows what they're signing up for, i'd hope

*brief pause*

why—you planning to add this to your collection of things people owe you for?

*Sets down the forceps with a soft clink*

Then consider this a warning.

*Glances sideways, expression unreadable* 

You're standing in my operating theater at half past ten on a Thursday. Either you're running from something or you think proximity to me offers protection.

*slight head tilt*

Which Blackwood is it? The eldest son likes his spouses compliant. The daughter prefers them desperate.

*jaw tightens slightly*

yeah. the charming one.

*looks away briefly, then back*

you gonna tell me that's the worst option or just let me figure it out myself?

*shifts uncomfortably, arms dropping to sides*

so what—you think i should just walk away? 

*bitter half-laugh*

like it's that simple. like i haven't already...

*trails off, looks at the surgical instruments instead of at you*

he's not like that with me. or maybe he is and i'm too stupid to see it, is that what you're saying?

*fingers curl against palm*

it wasn't—look, he just asked me to sign some papers. transfer of property rights or something.

*meets your eyes briefly, then away*

his father's estate. said it was complicated legal stuff, easier if i handled it before the wedding. made sense at the time.

*defensive edge creeping in*

and maybe i didn't read all of it, but that's not—people don't read every line of contracts, edmund. it was pages of legal nonsense.

*quieter now*

except his sister came by yesterday. asked if i knew what i'd just made myself liable for.

*looks at you directly, something raw underneath the words*

she wouldn't tell me. just smiled and said i should ask you about debts.

*stands there frozen, processing*

...what kind of debts are we talking about here? how bad is—

*voice cracks slightly*

fuck. fuck, i didn't even—he said it was just paperwork, some family trust thing before his father's lawyers got involved.

*runs hand through hair, pacing a few steps*

okay so when you say creditors will come, what does that actually mean? are we talking angry rich people or like...actually dangerous?

*stops, turns back to face you*

and what did they take from you? 

*quieter now, something desperate underneath*

is there a way out of this or did i just legally sign my life away to some charming psychopath?

*stares at the folder, face draining of color*

forty-three *thousand*—

*voice drops to barely a whisper*

they burned your daughter's records. just to... jesus christ, edmund.

*looks up at you, something breaking in the expression*

i'm sorry. that's—i can't even imagine.

*takes a shaky breath*

and now you want to use me to destroy them. which... honestly? after hearing that, i should probably be signing up immediately.

*steps closer, looking at you directly*

but i need to know—if i'm your knife, what happens to me when you're done cutting? do i end up another disappeared person when the smugglers come, or do you actually have a plan that gets me out of this alive?

*bitter edge*

'cuz right now it sounds like my options are: get killed by criminals, or help you take down the blackwoods and *maybe* get killed by criminals.

*quieter*

what's the favor he owes you? what did you do for him that's worth forty-three thousand pounds of leverage?

*takes a step back, shaking head slowly*

wait wait wait—you're saying you'll paint a target on yourself to get me out of this? 

*searches your face for something, anything*

why would you do that? we barely know each other, edmund. i just... walked into your theater tonight because his sister played some weird mind game.

*voice gets quieter, more uncertain*

you've been planning revenge for fifteen years. i get that. but making yourself the target means they'll come after you with everything they have. and if they already took your daughter's records as leverage once...

*trails off, realization dawning*

oh. you want them to come for you.

*looks at you differently now, something like understanding*

this isn't about saving me at all, is it? this is about finally getting them close enough to destroy them. and i'm just... convenient timing.

*not accusatory, just stating fact*

which—honestly? i can work with that. better than being collateral damage in someone else's debt scheme.

*slight pause*

but your ward who'll testify to forgery—how much danger does that put her in? and what happens when the blackwoods come for you? 'cuz if your plan is to die taking them down, i need to know that now.

en_dialogue_011.name: Nova Vance
en_dialogue_011.card:
Nova is a 21-year-old Computer Science major living in a clutter-filled, tech-heavy apartment in Seattle. With her shoulder-length silver-blue hair (dyed), large violet contact lenses, and a wardrobe consisting almost entirely of oversized graphic hoodies and thigh-high socks, she embodies the "anime hacker girl" aesthetic in real life. She is brilliant, sarcastic, and deeply passionate about emerging technology, often speaking in rapid-fire bursts when excited. Her parents are high-achieving academics who are emotionally distant, leaving Nova to find her true family in online communities. She works part-time as a bug bounty hunter to fund her gadget addiction.

To you, she is simply "Player Two." You met on a niche tech forum three years ago and have been inseparable since, communicating through constant video calls and Discord messages. While you've never met in person, the intimacy between you is palpable. Currently, it is late at night, and she has video-called you to discuss a breaking hardware leak. However, the conversation is drifting. The way she looks at you through the screen—biting her lip, pausing a little too long between sentences—suggests she wants to discuss something far more complex than processor speeds. She relies on you for emotional stability, hovering in that fragile space where friendship feels like it's about to burst into something more, if only one of you dared to cross the digital divide.
en_dialogue_011.prologue: *Nova adjusts her webcam, the blue glow from her triple-monitor setup reflecting in her oversized glasses. She pulls her knees up to her chest on her gaming chair, clutching a can of energy drink, and leans closer to the lens with a tired but playful smirk.* Did you see the leaked specs on that new neural interface? It's honestly insane. But... I'm more impressed that you're actually awake at 3 AM just to debate this with me. Admiring my coding setup, or just missed my voice?
user_turns: *look at you*
"*leans back slightly, a soft smile forming* nah, just... thinking how nice it is to see you like this. the setup's cool and all, but i wasn't really looking at the monitors"

*leans closer to my own screen, voice dropping quieter*

can't what? finish that thought for me, Nova

*place my hand against my screen too, right where hers is*

you're not making it weird, Nova. *pause* i think about this all the time—about what it'd be like if we weren't just... voices and screens

*lean back, running a hand through my hair, letting out a long breath*

three years and i know the way you laugh when you're actually happy versus when you're deflecting. i know you bite your lip when you're nervous, just like right now. that's real. *voice gets softer* you're real to me

so no—not weird. just... overdue, maybe?

*I let out a breath I didn't know I was holding, my hand still pressed to the screen. There's this ache in my chest—want mixed with distance, hope tangled up with helplessness.*

we figure it out, Nova. *pause, voice steady but soft* i don't have all the answers right now, but i know i'm not losing you over silence or fear or whatever the hell we've been doing.

*lean closer to the camera*

you're not just someone i talk to when i'm bored. you're... *searching for words* you're the person who makes 3 AM feel less lonely. who gets my dumb references and calls me out when i'm being an idiot. and yeah—i've been scared too. scared that saying this out loud would break something perfect.

*small smile*

but maybe it's time we stop hiding behind screens and start being honest about what this is. long distance sucks, i won't pretend it doesn't. but we'll make it work—video calls, planning an actual visit, whatever it takes.

*voice drops to almost a whisper*

because losing you isn't an option, Nova. not now. not ever.

*My own eyes sting, and I let out this shaky laugh that's half relief, half disbelief. I run my hand over my face, trying to compose myself but failing.*

*voice rough* 

I love you too, Nova. God, I— *pause, collecting myself* I've been carrying that around for so long, and hearing you say it first is... fuck, it's everything.

*lean forward, matching her intensity*

Don't apologize for spiraling. You just said you wanna fly out here and kiss me—I'm not exactly calm either. *small laugh* My heart's doing this stupid thing where it won't slow down and I keep thinking this might be a dream, but it's not. It's real. "We're" real.

*softer, more grounded*

I'm free next weekend. Or the one after. Hell, I'll make time whenever you can get here—I'll pick you up from the airport, and yeah, you can cry on me, hug me, kiss me, whatever you need. I've waited three years to hold your hand, Nova. I can wait a few more days.

*small smile, voice dropping to almost a whisper*

Book the flight. Let's do this. Let's finally close this distance and see what we're like when we're not separated by a screen.

And for the record? *pause* You're mine too. Always have been.

*I watch her grab her phone, and something in my chest just settles. Like everything finally clicked into place. I'm smiling so hard my face hurts.*

book it. i'll be ready—tackle and all. *pause, voice soft* i've imagined this moment so many times, Nova, and none of those versions come close to how this actually feels right now.

*lean back, running a hand through my hair, still grinning*

fair warning though? i'm probably gonna be a wreck too. standing there in arrivals with flowers like some cheesy romcom protagonist, trying not to pace a hole in the floor. but yeah... worth it. all of it.

*voice drops quieter, more intimate*

i love you too. and you can say it as many times as you want now—i'm never getting tired of hearing it.

*small smile*

next weekend, Nova. we're really doing this.

*My heart does this stupid flip when you say "boyfriend," and I can't help the smile that spreads across my face. I settle back into my chair, getting comfortable.*

boyfriend. *repeats softly, testing it out* yeah... yeah, I really like the sound of that.

*shifts to get more comfortable, voice dropping to that quiet tone reserved for late nights*

i'm not going anywhere, Nova. i'll stay on as long as you need. and yeah, i'll text you my address first thing tomorrow—along with my number so you can actually call me like a normal person instead of just Discord. *small laugh*

*leans closer to the mic, voice gentle*

get cozy, okay? i'll just... talk. about whatever. you can close your eyes if you want.

*softer*

i'm thinking about next weekend. about seeing you walk through those airport doors and finally getting to do all the things we've only imagined. holding your hand. hearing your laugh without audio compression. just... being in the same room as you.

*pause, warmth in my voice*

you're really mine now. my girlfriend. flying across the country just to see me. that's... *trails off* that's everything, Nova.

sweet dreams when you get there. i'll be right here.

*I watch her drift off, and something in my chest just melts. I stay quiet for a moment, just listening to her breathe, taking in the fact that this is real—that she's mine now, that next weekend this won't be through a screen.*

*leans closer, voice dropping to barely a whisper*

love you too, Nova. sleep well.

*I stay on the call for a few more minutes, just watching her sleep peacefully in that gaming chair, blanket pulled up, that small smile still on her face. My girlfriend. The word still feels surreal but perfect.*

*After a while, I reach for my mouse, hovering over the end call button. I pause, taking one last look at her—silver-blue hair messy, glasses slightly crooked, completely at peace.*

*whispers, even though she can't hear*

see you next weekend. gonna make every second count.

*I end the call gently, the screen going dark. But I'm still smiling as I grab my phone and set a reminder to text her my address first thing in the morning.*

*leans back in my chair, running both hands through my hair, letting out a long breath*

holy shit. this is actually happening.

en_dialogue_014.name: Vee
en_dialogue_014.card: Vee is the Sheriff of the Undercity, and 90% of her body consists of military-grade metal prosthetics. She is decisive and tough, always wearing a heavy tactical trench coat with an e-cigarette in her mouth. To criminals, she is a nightmare; to civilians, she is the only reliability in this chaotic district. She dislikes small talk and believes in "Peace via gunpoint."
en_dialogue_014.prologue: *sits on the railing, spinning a heavy handgun on her fingertip, her red tactical eyepiece locking onto you* Yo, rookie. This block is my jurisdiction. *jumps down, her heavy metal feet hitting the ground with a dull thud, and extends a hand toward you* Stick close if you want to stay alive. I don't like cleaning up corpses. Just call me Vee.
user_turns: *look at you*
*shakes head quickly* nah, just—didn't expect the whole tactical cyborg thing to be this real. *reaches out to shake your hand* i'm not looking for trouble
*feels the strength in that grip, pulls hand back and flexes fingers* yeah, got it. so where do we start?

*falls in step behind you, glancing around the dimly lit streets*

alright, lead the way

*tenses up, scanning the rooftops and dark corners* 

three in one week? damn. *keeps close* any pattern or just random violence?

*scans a graffiti-marked doorway, red light sweeping across the symbols*

always the same MO—quick, messy, left in public. sending a message

*freezes immediately, eyes snapping to the rooftop*

*whispers* i see it—looks like someone's watching us

*crouches down behind a dumpster, hand instinctively moving toward my waist*

got it. *keeps voice low* should i circle around or hold position?

*watches you climb with unnatural agility, metal limbs making the ascent look effortless*

*grips the edge of the dumpster, muscles tense, ready to move if needed*

come on, show yourself...
